vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit) is present. P2/P4 (Black Pot with eggshell) is present. P4 (Silver Guest) is not clearly identifiable as a distinct specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf remains stable; white rabbit anchor intact.
plant_audit.P4: Single leaf specimen present in the eggshell-topped pot; appears to be a remnant of a previous occupant.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture levels across all pots; no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P4/P2 area).
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clear, except for a cable intrusion in the current frame.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, the Pothos (P3) has remained static. The other pots show no signs of active growth or recovery. The eggshell fragment has remained in a fixed position.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The Pothos (P3) is in a state of stasis. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively dormant or deceased, showing no signs of vegetative vitality.
vision_report.anomalies: Introduction of a white cable crossing the frame in the current image. Presence of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment/top dressing.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the biome. The Pothos in the rabbit-anchored pot remains the only stable biological entity. The remaining pots are currently devoid of active, healthy plant life, suggesting a systemic failure of the other specimens. The environment is stable but stagnant.
